trivia_bullets.text: Case of the Burning Masks is a red Case enchantment in MKM's Case cycle. The 'burning masks' title invokes both the Rakdos Cult's performance-art tradition (fire, theatricality, masks) and the noir-mystery conceit of identities being burned away or exposed. As a red card in a set predominantly associated with the Azorius, Dimir, and Orzhov guilds, its Rakdos-tonal imagery aligns with Judith's subplot within the MKM story arc. [Scryfall]

The solve condition — three or more sources you controlled dealt damage this turn — is aggressive-deck friendly, rewarding wide burn or token-attack strategies over a single instance of damage. Once solved, the card is sacrificed to exile the top three library cards and play one of them that turn — effectively a one-shot Treasure Map payoff for aggro-spellslinger strategies. Legal in Standard, Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander. [Scryfall]

The art (by Bastien L. Deharme) depicts a burning interior framed by a dark doorway — the viewer observes catastrophe through a crack, voyeuristically. No flavor text on this card. The compositional choice — the crime scene as framed aperture — mirrors the Case mechanic's procedural logic: you're investigating from the outside, gathering evidence until the case is closed. [Scryfall]
